hero.question: Will Rory McIlroy win the 2026 Masters tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 17.5%, the crowd is pricing McIlroy as a tier-two Masters contender but the 11-point spike in 24 hours suggests fresh money betting on his window closing after back-to-back disappointing seasons. The real tell is whether his swing changes stick by spring 2026, because if they do, 17.5% drastically undervalues a player who still has the raw talent to win majors and faces a weaker field than he did in peak years.

daily_take.headline: McIlroy's Masters Odds Just Jumped 11 Points. Here's Why You Should Fade It.
daily_take.deck: Rory's 2026 Masters odds spiked to 17.5% on fresh volume, likely noise from recent PGA news and his name-value appeal. But 17.5% is too generous for a guy who hasn't won a major in a decade and still hasn't mastered Augusta's specifics. Smart money fades the sentiment spike and shorts the hype.

sidebar.headline: Iran strike odds collapsed 22 points in 24 hours, signaling real deescalation momentum. But 13.5% still prices in too much tail risk given recent diplomatic signals.
sidebar.label: UAE strikes Iran: 13.5%

sidebar.headline: Twins favored at 26.5% but the 28-point drop screams position unwinding, not fundamental shift. Check the recent injury reports before the line stabilizes.
sidebar.label: Twins over Tigers: 26.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic odds barely moved despite the geopolitical fireworks around Iran. The 25.5% level looks mispriced if UAE strikes happen, which just got less likely.
sidebar.label: Hormuz normalcy by April 30: 25.5%
